AlertTone.description: Alert tone (used by Alert, Toast, AnnouncementBar, AsideNote).

oneOf.description: Sign in with SSO single-sign-on link.

ChromeKind.description: Page-shell chrome kind. Picks the header + body-backdrop shape. Each variant is a complete chrome aesthetic, not a modifier on the same shell. Operators pick per-page via `CmsPage::chrome`; new sites typically pick `FloatingPill`, legacy sites stay on `PageShell` for backward compat.

oneOf.description: Sticky-blur top bar with brand-left / nav-right and an auto-h1 underneath. Three-radial-gradient body backdrop tinted by the active theme's accents. The default for backward compatibility.

Floating capsule: brand + nav links + action CTAs in a glass-morphism pill anchored at the top of the viewport, centered horizontally. Body backdrop is a single soft halo above the hero + a flat surface below.
No header at all. The first section carries every chrome element itself. Used for full-bleed landing pages.

CmsBannerTone.description: Banner tone — closed enum mirroring the standard color roles. Each maps to a `data-tone` attribute the skin styles.

oneOf.description: Yellow-tinted; use for time-sensitive / actionable notices.
Red-tinted; use for errors / critical alerts.

CmsCard.description: One feed card inside a [`CmsSection::CardFeed`]. Self-contained; no further nesting allowed in v1.

avatar.description: Avatar slot. Uses the same shape as the Composer avatar so downstream Loom CSS can share the circle treatment.

data_backend.description: Backend key (verified by phase_phantom_button at build time).

host.description:
Optional sub-line (e.g. "@court_dax · 4d left"). Rendered as `

` below the title.

href.description: Primary card link target. Validated by `is_safe_url`.

media.description: T70a: optional 16:9 media slot rendered ABOVE the body. When None, no media block is emitted (card is pure text). When Some, a `<div class="loom-card-feed-item__media">` wraps the asset (image / picture / video). Lazy-load via loading="lazy". Object-fit:cover via skin.css.

stats.description: Stats grid below the body. Empty → no grid emitted.

tag.description: Optional category tag (small badge above the title).

tone.description: Optional `data-tone` value for the tag chip (curated palette in skin.css: violet/indigo/ocean/forest/amber/rose/ruby/ walnut/slate/teal). When set, drives the chip's bg/fg/border hue. Falls back to the site's primary brand color when None. Sites that need a custom hue should keep `tone` None and emit inline `style="--tag-color: …"` via a future field.

CmsCardMedia.description: T70a: media slot for [`CmsCard`]. Three shapes: - `Image { src, alt, srcset?, width?, height? }` — `` - `Video { poster?, src, type, alt }` — native `

alt.description: REQUIRED accessible-name text. Empty string allowed only for purely decorative media (then aria-hidden will be set by the renderer too).

width.description: Optional intrinsic dimensions (drives layout-shift avoidance via the rendered width/height attrs).

oneOf.description: Native HTML `<video>`. No autoplay. controls=true. preload=metadata.

mime.description: MIME type — must be one of: video/mp4, video/webm, video/ogg.

oneOf.description: Visible-but-empty media area. CSS gradient placeholder.

CmsCardStat.description: One {label, value} pair inside a [`CmsCard`]'s stats grid.

CmsCodeShellLine.description: One line of a `CodeShell` transcript. Owned-string variant of the `loom_components` primitive's `CodeShellLine<'a>`.

text.description: Line text. Rendered as-is via Maud (auto-escaped).

CmsCodeShellLineKind.description: Semantic role of a single line in [`CmsSection::CodeShell`]. Mirrors `loom_components::code_shell::CodeShellLineKind`.

oneOf.description: User-typed shell command. Renders with the prompt prefix.
Program output. Indented to align with the command text.
Annotation by the author. Dimmed + italic, prefixed with `#`.
Error line. Picks up the warn / error color.

colophon.description: Optional copyright / colophon line at the very bottom.

columns.description: Footer link columns. Common shape: 3-5 columns, each with a heading and a vertical list of links.

contact.description: Optional contact-info block (phone / email / address / jurisdiction). Renders as a separate column on the right.

legal_links.description: Bottom-row legal links (Privacy, Terms, Imprint, etc.). Rendered as inline-flex under the columns.

heading.description: Column heading (rendered as small uppercase label).
Optional column heading (defaults to "Contact" if absent).

phone.description: Phone number (any format; renderer wraps in tel: link if the string starts with a digit or `+`).

CmsFormField.description: Typed form field. Closed enum — adding a variant requires a renderer arm + test.

hint.description: Optional hint paragraph below the input.

oneOf.description: User is currently on this step.
Step is ahead of the user.

data_backend.description: Backend key (verified by phantom_button at build time).

secondary_label.description: Optional secondary button label (e.g. "Save draft"). None → no secondary button rendered.

CmsKvItem.description:
FORGE_ROADMAP item 41: one entry in a [`CmsSection::KvPair`].

BUG ASSUMPTION: `key` and `value` carry no markup; renderer auto-escapes via Maud. `hint` is also escaped and rendered as a muted single-line caption — not a place for arbitrary HTML.

hint.description: Optional muted caption shown below the value.

key.description: Label / dt text. Auto-escaped on render.

value.description: Value / dd text. Auto-escaped on render.

CmsLogoItem.description:
T660 P1: one brand logo entry in a [`CmsSection::LogoWall`].

`name` is the canonical brand name + lookup key into the future `loom-brand-icons` registry. `href` is the brand's website. Until the registry crate lands, the renderer falls back to typographic rendering (the name in `loom-font-display`).

AVP-2: never carries inline SVG; the registry is the only path through which SVG markup reaches the page.

href.description: Brand website URL. Auto-escaped + rendered as the wrap element if non-empty.

name.description: Brand name (also the lookup key into `loom-brand-icons`). Examples: "Stripe", "Linear", "Vercel".

current.description: Mark this link as the current page (renders `aria-current="page"`).

data_backend.description: Backend key (verified against `backends.toml` by forge's phase_phantom_button).

CmsPanelBody.description: Typed panel body. Closed enum — adding a variant requires a renderer arm + test.

oneOf.description: Ordered list of `{label, value, href?}` rows. Each row renders as a `<li>`; if `href` is set + valid, the row is wrapped in an `<a>`.

Plain prose paragraph(s), each entry → one `

`.

data_backend.description: Optional backend key (verified by phantom_button at build).

CmsPromptAction.description: Closed enum mirror of [`PromptAction`] — separated so the wire format is independent of the Loom enum's internals.

CmsSection.description:
One section of a page. Adding a variant requires a paired renderer arm in [`render_section`] and a unit test.

**Doc convention for variants:** every variant carries a one-line docstring explaining its purpose. Inline-struct fields on each variant are self-documenting via their type signature + the JSON-schema name (e.g., `children_html: String` on `Container` is exactly what it says) — per-field docs are only added when a field's meaning is NOT obvious from `<name>: <type>` alone (constraints, units, encoding format). The blanket `#[allow(missing_docs)]` below avoids the maintenance tax of 300+ noise-tier doc comments on the catalogue expansion; new variants ARE expected to carry per-field docs for any non-self-evident shape.

oneOf.description: Top-of-page hero. Optional eyebrow pill, required title, optional lede, optional primary CTA. Loom-namespaced (no Tailwind dependency) so it composes cleanly with the SkillShots PoC skin.

eyebrow.description: Optional small pill above the title.

lede.description:
Optional subhead paragraph.

REGRESSION-GUARD: serde alias `subtitle` accepts the pre-2026-05 field name. Older fixture files written by the form-builder still use `subtitle`; without the alias the renderer 500s on read because `deny_unknown_fields` rejects unknown keys. Save-path scrubbing handles the write side; this handles the read side. Cycle 52 /preview-edit/about.html 500 was the trigger.

oneOf.description:
Editorial asymmetric hero — the substrate-aware sibling of [`CmsSection::Hero`]. Where `Hero` ships the canonical centered SaaS shape (eyebrow pill, gradient overlay, animate-fade-in chain), `HeroEditorial` is the editorial 2-column composition: monospace kicker, dominant headline, lede that sets its own measure via `max-w-prose`, optional CTA, optional right-column decoration. No SaaS-trope ornaments emitted.

Wire shape mirrors [`loom_components::HeroEditorial`]; the CMS authors a section, the renderer emits editorial markup.

headline.description: Headline body. Renders as the dominant typographic mass.

headline_accent.description: Optional accent fragment rendered after the headline in brand primary color.

kicker.description: Plain uppercase monospace metadata line above the headline (e.g., `"DISPATCH · 2026-05-20"`). NOT a pill.

lede.description: Lede paragraph. Use long-form editorial prose; the renderer lets prose set its own measure.

oneOf.description: Group: a heading + a body paragraph, framed as a `<section>`. Useful for "How it works" / "Rules" type explainer blocks.

body.description:
Body paragraph(s). Each entry becomes a `

`.

title.description: Heading text (rendered as h2 inside the section).

oneOf.description: Card-feed: an ordered list of feed cards (battle list, leaderboard rows, vote queue, etc.). Each card carries an avatar, title, optional host subline, optional stats grid, and primary link. Maps to a series of `<article class="loom-card-feed-item">` inside a `<section class="loom-card-feed">`.

heading.description: Optional heading rendered above the list.

oneOf.description: Sidebar: a stack of typed `Panel`s rendered inside an `<aside>` landmark. Used for index.html's right-rail (top earners / open votes / house rules / etc.).

label.description: Optional aria-label for the `<aside>` landmark. Defaults to "Side panels" if omitted.

panels.description: Stack of panels, top to bottom.

oneOf.description: Top-of-page persistent banner. Used for "voting closes in 1h", "maintenance Saturday 2-4am", "PoC build" type notices. Renders as an `<aside>` with a tone-tinted background and an optional close button.

dismissible.description: If true, render a close button with `data-loom-banner-dismiss` (client JS handles).

id.description: Optional stable id used by client JS to remember a dismissal across page loads.

tone.description: Visual tone — info / warn / success / danger.

oneOf.description: Multi-step form. Renders as `<form>` with a step indicator at top + each step's fields below + a submit row at bottom. Used for post-skill.html upload flow.

legend.description: Form heading (rendered as h2 inside the section).

steps.description: Ordered steps (multi-page UX rendered single-page in the SSG output; client JS can swap visibility later).

oneOf.description: Single image with the full Picture treatment.

alt.description: Required alt text. Empty string only for decorative.

oneOf.description:
A paragraph of body text. Maud auto-escapes on render.

`decoration` is an optional editorial nudge that bumps the paragraph out of "default body" into one of three named editorial shapes (lead / drop_cap / aside). Pure visual — the text content is unchanged.

A heading. Level constrained to 2-6 (h1 is owned by the page-shell template, not section content). T36 (2026-05-14): `level` is a typed `HeadingLevel` enum so out-of-range values are uncompilable AND parse-failed at the JSON boundary — no runtime clamp surface.

id.description:
Optional id attribute — lets the heading serve as a jump-link anchor target (`href="#that-id"`). Slug- validated via [`SlugName`] when present so we never emit a freeform attribute value into the DOM.

2026-05-20 substrate addition: without this slot, every page that uses `/page.html#section` in a nav link triggers a link-check phase strict-fail. Adding the slot lets cms authors declare a typed anchor next to the heading it labels.

level.description: Typed heading level. JSON: integer 2..=6 (anything else fails parse with `deny_unknown_fields`-style strictness).

polish.description: Bounded per-element typographic adjustments. Each listed token emits a `loom-polish--X` class on the heading element. Idempotent — duplicates collapse. See [`PolishToken`] for the closed enum of one-step adjustments.

oneOf.description: FORGE_ROADMAP item 41 — typed key/value list (definition-list shape). Renders as a `<dl>` with one `<dt>/<dd>` pair per item; optional `hint` shows as a muted span under the value. Use cases: "Settings", "Match details", "Spec sheet", "Receipt fields", "Profile facts" — anywhere a label-and-value row stack would otherwise be hand-rolled markup.

density.description: Layout density. Defaults to `comfortable`. Mirrors the loom-components KvPairCard density knob; affects vertical rhythm inside each item.

heading.description: Optional heading (rendered as h2 above the list).

tone.description: Color tone. Defaults to `slate`. `amoled` honors `[[dark-theme-amoled-true-black]]` for OLED rendering.

oneOf.description:
T660 P1 LogoWall — a wall of vetted brand logos used as social-proof on marketing landings ("Trusted by Stripe, Linear, Vercel, ..."). Surfaced in 4 of 5 T660 dogfood rebuilds — highest dedup-priority variant in the registry.

`items` carries explicit text labels because the actual SVG markup lives in a vetted `loom-brand-icons` registry crate (queued separately). Until that registry lands, the renderer emits a typographic placeholder; once it lands, the registry lookup happens at render time keyed by the label.

AVP-2: brand SVG bodies are TRUSTED content; this CmsSection never accepts inline SVG from user input.

heading.description: Optional heading rendered above the wall ("Trusted by", "Customers", etc.).

oneOf.description: T660 P2 Quote — testimonial card. Surfaced in 3 of 3 T660 marketing rebuilds (Stripe, Linear, Vercel) — second-highest dedup-priority. Single quote per section; multi-quote carousels = multiple Quote sections (deliberate, lets downstream operators reorder via the picker).

body.description: The quoted text. Auto-escaped on render.

oneOf.description: T660 P3 Code — fenced code or terminal-output block. Surfaced in 2 of 3 T660 marketing rebuilds (Stripe API callouts + Vercel `npx vercel` snippets). Renders as semantic `<pre><code class="language-<lang>">`; the typed `lang` field keeps callers honest about syntax-highlighting hints without shipping a runtime highlighter in v1.

body.description: Body of the block. Auto-escaped via Maud. Multi-line OK.

caption.description: Optional caption rendered above the block.

lang.description: Fence language hint. Empty = generic. Examples: "bash", "rust", "javascript", "terminal".

terminal.description: True if the block represents terminal/shell output (sets data-loom-terminal so the skin can render a chrome bar).

oneOf.description:
Typed-line terminal transcript — the substrate-aware sibling of [`CmsSection::Code`]. Where `Code` is a single flat body string with a `terminal` flag, `CodeShell` carries per-line semantic role annotation (Command / Output / Comment / Error) so the skin can color + indent each line by its meaning. Wire shape mirrors [`loom_components::CodeShell`].

Use cases: forge build audit-chain proof, configuration walk- throughs, terminal-style decoration inside a HeroEditorial.

prompt.description: Prompt prefix for `Command` lines. `None` defaults to `$`.

title.description: Header title — shell name, filename, or label. Only emitted when `chrome` is `Header`.

oneOf.description: Full-bleed image/gradient hero. Larger + more visually ambitious than [`CmsSection::Hero`]; spans the viewport width breaking out of the standard content max-width. Use for top-of-page landing sections that need atmosphere.

after_cta.description: Typed slot — sections that render BELOW the CTA. Use for trust-signal logos, disclaimer copy, secondary CTA chains, fine-print legal.

align.description:
Text + content alignment. Default is `Center` (SaaS hero posture). Editorial sites can opt out via `Start` for left-aligned headline + lede + cta — the substrate emits a `data-align` attribute the skin keys on.

2026-05-20 substrate-de-consumer-shaping addition. Northbrook Observatory ships with `align: "start"` to avoid the SaaS-trope centered hero.

before_headline.description: Typed slot — sections that render ABOVE the title. Use for trust signals, badges, version chips, announcement banners, additional eyebrow content. Each section in the slot renders in order. Closes #105 (slot-based composition).

eyebrow.description: Optional eyebrow chip above the title.

height.description: Visual height ramp. Affects min-height + padding.

lede.description: Optional lede paragraph below the title.

oneOf.description: Text + visual side-by-side hero. Text occupies one column, a typed visual (code snippet, single stat, or photo asset slug from `loom-assets`) occupies the other.

visual_right.description: True → visual on the right (default), false → left.

oneOf.description: Multi-column feature listing. Each item carries an icon (slug from `loom-assets`), a heading, a body, and an optional learn-more link. `columns` clamps to 1..=4.

columns.description: Column count (1..=4). Mobile collapses to 1 regardless.

decoration.description:
Visual treatment. `Decorated` (default, kept for backward compatibility) is the SaaS-card shape with rounded chrome, gradient icon tile, hover lift + shadow. `Editorial` strips card chrome down to typography + top accent rule. `Minimal` is a tight grid with no decoration.

Substrate-de-consumer-shaping: callers asking for dense editorial composition opt out of the trope chrome without inventing a parallel primitive.

heading.description: Optional section heading above the grid.

oneOf.description: Row of large animated numbers with labels. Used for "by the numbers" / "stats that matter" social-proof bands.

heading.description: Optional section heading above the row.

oneOf.description: Numbered process steps. Visual: tall vertical timeline on mobile, horizontal row on desktop.

items.description: Step items in order. The renderer numbers them 1..=N automatically.

oneOf.description: Typed pricing tier display. One band of side-by-side tier cards with the optional "highlighted" tier visually distinguished.
Accordion of question / answer pairs. Each item is expandable; only one expanded at a time when `single_expand` is true.

single_expand.description: Auto-collapse other open items when one is opened.

oneOf.description: Horizontally-scrolling band of short text or brand names. Used as a continuous-motion social-proof rail.

items.description: Items to scroll. Duplicated automatically for seamless looping.

oneOf.description: Full-width call-to-action band. Sits at the bottom of marketing pages just above the footer.

align.description:
Text + content alignment. Default is `Center` (SaaS CTA-band posture); editorial sites can opt out via `Start` for left-aligned. Same `HeroAlign` enum reused so the cms author has one mental model across hero + cta sections.

2026-05-20 substrate-de-consumer-shaping addition.

oneOf.description:
Editorial pull-quote. Editorial-mark composition — left-border rule, no card chrome, no decorative quote-mark glyphs. Distinct from `Quote` which is the testimonial-card shape.

Wire-extended in this commit to mirror [`loom_components::PullQuote`]: `cite_url`, `emphasis`, and `tone` ship as additive fields with serde defaults so the existing two-field shape (`body` + `attribution`) keeps parsing.

body.description:
Body of the quote. Multi-paragraph bodies split on `\n\n` into separate `

` tags inside the `

`.

cite_url.description: Optional source URL — rendered into the `cite=` attribute on `<blockquote>` per the HTML spec for machine-readable provenance.

oneOf.description: Editorial epigraph — an opening quote, poem fragment, or motto placed BEFORE the article's first paragraph. Distinct from [`CmsSection::PullQuote`] (which interrupts mid-flow) and from [`CmsSection::Quote`] (which is a generic quotation card). Sets the rhetorical tone for the piece.

Editorial sidenote — text that floats to the side at wide viewports and renders inline as a small offset block at narrow ones. Common in long-form essays (literary press, academic publication, deep technical doc). Distinct from Paragraph + Aside-decoration: marginalia is positioned relative to the body text, not styled in line.

`position` controls which side it floats to at wide viewports. At narrow viewports both render as a small inline block, regardless of side.
Account-summary card — typed surface for a logged-in user's at-a-glance state. Renders avatar + display name + plan + member-since. Read-only; editing flows through `ProfileEdit`. Server-rendered.

handle.description: Optional secondary line under the name (e.g. handle).

member_since.description: Member-since string (RFC 3339 date or human form).

oneOf.description: Profile-edit form — typed surface for the logged-in user to update their identity-facing fields. Distinct from `SettingsPanel` (which is preferences); ProfileEdit is "who you are." Server-rendered; posts back to caller's endpoint.

Terms-of-Service / Privacy-Policy / similar legal-doc page. Typed structure: title, last-updated, table-of- contents (auto-derived from sections), then a flat list of named sections with anchored headings + plain-language summary boxes.

plain_language_summary.description: Optional plain-language tl;dr above the table of contents.

oneOf.description: Settings / preferences panel — a typed surface for the logged-in user's site preferences. Renders as a labeled definition-list with controls (toggles / text / danger buttons), categorised. Server-side-rendered so it survives LOOM_NOSCRIPT_MODE; the form posts back to a tenant- provided endpoint (the renderer doesn't bind a handler).

action.description: Where the form POSTs on submit. Caller-side endpoint; the renderer just emits `action=`.

heading.description: Optional section heading rendered above the panel.

lede.description: Optional lede paragraph below the heading.

submit_label.description: Submit-button label. Defaults to "Save changes" if omitted at the JSON layer.

oneOf.description: N-column free-form layout (2/3/4 cols on desktop).
Typed grid container with col count.
Long-form article wrapper (sets max-width + reading type).
Large opening paragraph — sets the article's tone.

Second-tier subhead beneath the lede. Newspapers use this to extend the headline-and-lede pair with one more editorial beat before the body. Distinct from [`CmsSection::Lede`] (sets the tone) and from [`CmsSection::SubHeading`] (sections the body).

Editorial kicker — short uppercase label above a headline. Newspaper / magazine convention: "OPINION", "LIVE", "BREAKING", "Q&A", "REVIEW". Distinct from the eyebrow chip on a hero (which is a hero-internal slot); this is a standalone editorial label.

Byline — typed author / role / dateline / reading-time unit. Authors currently emit this as 3-4 separate Paragraphs; the typed unit lets the renderer position them together and lets the LFI Critic verify a piece has a coherent attribution block.

dateline.description: Publication or last-updated dateline (ISO 8601 or human-readable; the renderer treats it as free text).

oneOf.description: End-of-document footnote. Renders as a numbered entry at the bottom of a long-form piece. Distinct from [`CmsSection::Footnote`] (which is inline / mid-flow); use `Endnote` when the author wants all annotations grouped.

Renderer-supplied "fact about Loom" — a typed slot that expands to the current value at render time so cms/*.json authors never hand-write counts that go stale.

Example: cms author writes `{"kind": "loom_fact", "which": "primitive_count", "shape": "inline"}` and the renderer emits the current count (e.g. `170`). When new primitives ship, every cms page using this slot updates automatically.

Closes the "hand-authored 132 vs 170 vs 166" staleness pattern that hit `cms/{about,blog,docs,index,platform, pricing,why}.json` earlier this loop.

shape.description: How to wrap the value. `Inline` for `{count}` flow; `Sentence` for `"170 typed primitives ship today."`

oneOf.description: Figure with caption + optional credit line.
Image caption text (used outside a figure).
Glossary — sorted definition list with anchored terms.
Pull-out single big stat inline in editorial.

decoration.description:
Visual treatment. `Decorated` (default, back-compat) is the legacy avatar+quote card; `Editorial` drops the card chrome and renders as pull-quote-style typography; `Minimal` is just the quote + attribution line, no card, no avatar.

PRIORITY 6 audit — `.loom-testimonial` had textbook "fake testimonial card with avatars" trope CSS (rounded chrome + circular avatar). Variant-aware opt-out lets sites use the primitive without committing to that shape.

oneOf.description: Richer logo cloud with grayscale + hover-color treatment.
Transient toast (visible target for live regions).
Modal dialog placeholder (rendered as a typed section).
Typed sign-in / sign-up card. Holds an ordered list of authentication method choices the renderer expands into passkey buttons / social-auth rows / password fields / magic-link inputs.

factor.description: Factor kind shown to the user.

instructions.description: Operator-facing instructions ("Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app").

otp_length.description: Expected code length for OTP factors (6 for TOTP, 8 for backup codes).

oneOf.description: The embeddable Crucible captcha challenge widget.

attribution_hint.description: Attribution-policy hint for the user about how their response may be used.

option_count.description: Number of option slots the widget should render (e.g. 9 for a 3x3 image-classify grid).

prompt.description: Operator-facing prompt ("Select all photos with a bird", "Which of these sentences mean the same thing?").

oneOf.description: Stepper for multi-step auth flows (sign-up → verify email → add MFA → complete profile).
Compact "signed in as / sign out" footer.
No header — just the code block, framed by a single border.
Text-only header showing the shell name or filename. No traffic-light circles, no gradient.
Slate-100 ink on AMOLED true-black surface.

ContentWidth.description: Content-width preference for `main#content`. Wires through to a `data-content-width="X"` attribute on `<body>` that skin.css matches with `main#content { max-inline-size: ... }`.

CrucibleKind.description: Crucible challenge kind mirror (kept independent from crucible-core to avoid creating a Loom-→-Crucible dep).

answer.description: Answer body — may contain multiple paragraphs.

FeatureSpotlightDecoration.description:
Visual treatment for [`CmsSection::FeatureSpotlight`].

The default `Decorated` is the legacy SaaS-card shape (rounded chrome + gradient icon tile + hover lift + shadow). The `Editorial` and `Minimal` variants strip the trope chrome for callers that want dense, non-card composition.

oneOf.description: SaaS-card shape: rounded chrome, gradient icon tile, hover lift + shadow. Default (kept for back-compat).
Strip card chrome. Typography only, no icon tile, no shadow. Top accent rule per item.
Tight grid, no decoration. Title + body, no icon, no border.

HeadingLevel.description: Heading level (h2-h6). h1 is reserved for the page-shell.

HeroAlign.description:
Text + content alignment for [`CmsSection::ImageHero`]. Default is `Center` (existing SaaS-hero posture); editorial sites opt out via `Start` for left-aligned headline + lede + cta.

Substrate-de-consumer-shaping doctrine: the substrate ships the posture; the cms author chooses whether to take it. Backwards compatible — existing CmsPage JSON that omits `align` keeps rendering centered.

oneOf.description: Headline + lede + cta centered. SaaS-marketing default.
Left-aligned editorial posture. Use for editorial content sites where centered marketing copy reads as ad-tier.
Animated three-radial gradient mesh in the accent palette. The default — works in every theme without configuration.
Solid color. `token` references a loom color token slug (e.g. `"loom-color-surface"`).
Diagonal-stripe pattern in the accent color.

Photographic background. `src` is a same-origin asset path (e.g. `"/assets/hero-bg.jpg"`). `alt` is the SEO/a11y description; even though the image is rendered as a CSS `background-image`, the alt text ships as `<meta>` / aria-attributes on the section.

Used by sites that lead with a real photograph (people, product, location) rather than a gradient halo. `overlay` controls the dark/light scrim on top of the photo so the title stays legible: `none` / `light` / `dark`.

src.description: Same-origin path to the image asset.

HeroCta.description: Hero CTA — the single typed primary action attached to a Hero section. URL is validated by `composer::is_safe_url` at render time.

data_backend.description: Backend key (must match a `[backends.X]` in backends.toml). Forge's phantom_button phase verifies this at build time.

href.description: Where the CTA navigates. Same-origin path or https://.

HeroEditorialBackground.description:
Background tone for [`CmsSection::HeroEditorial`].

Mirrors `loom_components::hero::HeroEditorialBackground`. `Slate` is the recommended default — lets content carry the page. `Amoled` honors `[[dark-theme-amoled-true-black]]` for OLED pixels-off rendering.

oneOf.description: Tall — about 80vh, only for top-of-funnel landings.

KvPairDensity.description: Layout density for [`CmsSection::KvPair`]. Mirrors `loom_components::card::KvPairDensity`. Affects vertical rhythm inside each item; horizontal sizing is the grid's job.

oneOf.description: Tight rhythm — for grids of 4-6 items with short values.
Generous rhythm — for grids of 2-3 items with long values.
Slate-100 ink on AMOLED true-black surface.

LegalSection.description: One section of a [`CmsSection::LegalDoc`]. Each section gets a stable kebab-case anchor (derived from `heading`) so the auto-generated table of contents can link to it.

body.description:
Body paragraphs (plain text, auto-escaped). Each entry renders as a `

` in order.

plain_language.description: Optional plain-language summary callout box inside this section.

LoomFactKind.description: Which Loom fact to inject via [`CmsSection::LoomFact`]. Closed enum so authors can't ask for a fact the renderer doesn't know.

oneOf.description: Total count of named themes shipped (light, dark, dark-amoled, auto, warm, ocean, forest, violet, rose, sepia, press, hc-dark, hc-light, and any future named theme).
Forge audit-phase count. Renderer reports the value Loom believes Forge ships; if Forge's count diverges, bump the constant.
Multi-network DeployAdapter count (Tor / I2P / Lokinet / IPFS / Gemini / Clearnet).

Just the numeric value, no markup. For inline use inside existing prose: `"At {{loom_fact}} typed primitives..."` — but since Loom doesn't do template strings, `Inline` here means rendering as a `<span>` so callers can place it in a Paragraph slot.
A complete short sentence: `"170 typed Loom primitives ship today."`
Float to the right of the body text (start of inline-axis in RTL locales). Default — least visually-disruptive because it occupies the gutter on the reading-direction side.
Float to the left of the body text.
Time-based OTP from an authenticator app.

ParagraphDecoration.description:
Editorial treatment for a [`CmsSection::Paragraph`]. All variants render `

` — the difference is class-keyed CSS: * `Body` (default) — standard body text. No extra class. * `Lead` — larger leading paragraph (the lede after a hero). Renders with `class="loom-paragraph--lead"`. * `DropCap` — first letter rendered as a multi-line drop cap. Renders with `class="loom-paragraph--dropcap"`. * `Aside` — visually offset commentary; left-rule, slightly muted color. Renders with `class="loom-paragraph--aside"`. Pure decoration. Text content is unaffected.

oneOf.description: Default body text. No extra class.
First letter rendered as a multi-line drop cap.
Visually offset commentary — left rule + muted color.

PhotoOverlay.description: Overlay tint applied on top of [`HeroBackground::Photo`] so the title remains legible.

oneOf.description: Light overlay (for dark photos / dark-text titles).
Dark overlay (for bright photos / light-text titles). Default.

PolishToken.description:
T36: typed heading level for `CmsSection::Heading`.

`<h1>` is reserved for the page-shell template (every page has exactly one); section headings live at h2..=h6. The variants here mirror the HTML tag set 1:1 — there is no `H1` variant because emitting a second h1 from CMS content would break landmark semantics.

JSON wire format: integer `2..=6`. `serde` round-trips through `u8`; out-of-range values fail `Deserialize` at the boundary with a clear error, never landing as runtime drift.

Why typed instead of `u8`: * **Compile-time guarantee** — `HeadingLevel::H7` doesn't compile. Runtime clamps + `data-cms-warn` markers no longer needed; the level is always valid by construction. * **Exhaustive match** — every consumer matches on the enum and the compiler refuses to forget a variant. * **Type-state doctrine** — moves a runtime invariant into the type system, where AVP-2's "no boolean blindness" rule belongs. Heading level encoded over the wire as a raw u8 (2..=6) — the `serde(into / try_from)` pair lets derive produce the same wire shape as the prior hand-rolled impls (rejected by the composition Per-element bounded typographic adjustment. Each variant nudges one default in one direction. Substrate guarantees each adjustment is exactly ONE step from default — no compounding, no unbounded styling. Authors who want stronger emphasis pick a different primitive (e.g. PullQuote for dramatic body text, ImageHero for marketing title weight).

Maps to a `loom-polish--<kebab>` class on the rendered element. CSS rules are caller-side; substrate ships sensible defaults via skin.css.

Per `feedback_consumer_shaped_substrate`: polish tokens let authors express "this specific element deserves a bit more weight" without unbounded CSS — a structural compromise that keeps the substrate's variation policy bounded.

PricingTier.description: One pricing tier in a [`CmsSection::Pricing`] section.

badge.description: Optional badge label displayed on the tier card (e.g. "Most popular", "Best value", "Compliance-ready"). `None` → no badge; the hardcoded English "Popular" CSS fallback is gone (substrate-de-consumer-shaping + localization fix).

highlighted.description: True → tier is visually highlighted (typically the middle "recommended" tier).

oneOf.description: Slate-100 ink on AMOLED true-black surface.

SettingsControl.description: Typed control for a [`SettingsItem`]. Closed enum; adding a variant is a deliberate surface change.

oneOf.description: Destructive action button. Renders separately from the main form submit — its own POST endpoint + a confirm prompt at the form level.

action.description: Where the danger action POSTs. Distinct from the panel's main `action`.

confirm_text.description: Confirm-prompt text — rendered as the form's `aria-describedby` content + as a visual disclaimer.

SettingsItem.description: One item in a [`SettingsCategory`]. Each item pairs a label with a control. Controls are typed via `SettingsControl`.

hint.description: Optional muted hint shown below the label.

label.description: Visible label rendered as the `<dt>` for the row.

name.description: Form-input name attribute — keys the value in the submitted form.

oneOf.description: A code snippet, rendered as a styled `<pre>` panel.
A single big stat with label.
Reference to a `loom-assets` photo / illustration slug.

hint.description: Optional muted hint below the label.

value.description: Value — displayed as the large headline.

title.description: Step heading (renderer auto-prefixes the step number).

TabItem.description: One tab in a Tabs section.

TestimonialDecoration.description:
Visual treatment for [`CmsSection::Testimonial`].

`Decorated` (default) is the legacy avatar+quote card. `Editorial` drops card chrome for pull-quote typography. `Minimal` is just the quote + attribution line.

oneOf.description: Quote + attribution only. No card, no avatar.

description: A single CMS-managed page. The smallest unit the bridge knows how to render in isolation.

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference. Editors with jsonls (VS Code, Helix, Zed) read this to provide inline autocomplete + validation. The bridge ignores the value — it's the editor's contract, not the renderer's. Authors should generate the schema via `loom cms-schema --out ...` and reference it here as `"$schema": "../cms-schema.json"`.

brand.description: Top-of-page brand label rendered as the `loom-page-brand` link. When omitted, the renderer derives a brand from the first segment of `title` before " — " / " · " / "—" / " - " separators.

chrome.description: Page-shell chrome style. Picks the header / body-backdrop shape. Default = `PageShell` (legacy SkillShots-style sticky bar). Other variants: - `FloatingPill` — modern floating capsule centered on top of the viewport with glass-morphism backdrop. Drops the cream three-radial-gradient body backdrop. - `Minimal` — no header at all; first section carries all chrome.

content_width.description: Maximum inline-size of `main#content`. Closes the hard-coded `max-width: 64rem` page-shell default flagged in docs/SUBSTRATE_DE_CONSUMER_SHAPING_AUDIT.md. Default `Comfortable` matches the previous hard-coded value; `Narrow` is editorial-press measure; `Wide` is for dense-grid content; `Full` removes the cap entirely.

dev_devtools.description:
When true, page-shell emits a relaxed CSP (drops Trusted Types, allows 'unsafe-inline' styles) and injects a localStorage-gated loader script that pulls `/eruda.min.js` on demand. Off by default; turn on via the per-page JSON flag or via Forge's `FORGE_DEV_DEVTOOLS` env var (which forge-phases/render.rs reads and patches onto every page in a build).

SECURITY: only enable on dev hosts. Prod pages must keep the strict CSP. The page renders identically with or without devtools — the only delta is CSP + a tiny loader script that does nothing unless the visitor sets `localStorage["loom_eruda"] = "on"`. So even if a dev page ships to a stranger by mistake, they see no devtools (and no script load) without that opt-in.

footer.description: Optional typed footer. When `None`, the page-shell emits an empty `<footer class="loom-page-footer"></footer>` (back-compat). When `Some`, the renderer expands a typed multi-column footer with optional contact info and legal links.

nav_actions.description: Action CTAs rendered in the header (e.g. "Sign in", "Get started"). Distinct from `nav_links` — these are buttons, not page-to-page links.

nav_links.description: Optional primary navigation links. The page-shell renders these inside `<nav aria-label="Primary">`. Empty/omitted → shell emits brand-only nav. Each link's `href` is validated (same-origin path or `https://`); invalid hrefs render as `#invalid-nav-link` placeholders.

path.description: Canonical URL path (e.g. `"/leaderboard"`). Required. Used by the layout shell to emit `<link rel="canonical">`.

sections.description: Sequence of body sections, top to bottom.

site_origin.description: Optional fully-qualified site origin (e.g. `"https://dev.plausiden.com"`). When set, the renderer prefixes `og:url` and `og:image` with this origin so social-card crawlers see fully-qualified URLs (the OG spec requires absolute URLs). When `None`, the renderer emits the relative path — works for most crawlers but not all.

social_image.description: Optional social-card preview image path (e.g. `"/assets/photos/stock-80.jpg"`). When set, the renderer emits `og:image` and `twitter:image` meta tags. When the page also has `site_origin` set, the path is prefixed to produce a fully-qualified URL.

theme.description: Theme name. Routed through page_shell_themed's `data-theme` attribute. Closed allowlist: `light`, `dark`, `auto`, `warm`, `ocean`, `forest`, `violet`, `rose`. Operators pick per-page (or set once in the site's index.json to inherit).

title: CmsPage
